2605.22993.title: A Proactive Multi-Agent Dialogue Framework for Assessing Social Language Disorder Traits in Autism
2605.22993.paper_summary: Characteristic linguistic behaviors associated with Social Language Disorder (SLD) in autism spectrum disorder, including echoic repetition, pronoun displacement, and stereotyped media quoting, are largely absent from spontaneous conversation and only emerge under specific conversational conditions. In structured clinical assessments, this latency means that questioning strategy selection is a critical yet underappreciated determinant of how much diagnostic information a conversation yields. Whether large language models (LLMs) can be guided to proactively select questioning strategies that systematically surface these latent traits remains largely unexplored. Here we present TPA (Think, Plan, Ask), a proactive multi-agent dialogue framework applied to the language assessment component of the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule Module 4 (ADOS-2), in which a doctor agent explicitly reasons about which traits remain unobserved before selecting a clinically grounded strategy and generating a targeted question. A patient agent grounded in real ADOS-2 clinical data enables reproducible evaluation without real patient participation, validated across three independent experiments confirming adequate fidelity to real patient language. Evaluated on 484 episodes from 35 patients, TPA outperforms six competitive dialogue planning baselines across all primary metrics, achieving 82.1% SLD trait coverage, 16.6% higher than automated replay of real clinical dialogues conducted by trained clinicians (65.5%), with substantially greater per-turn diagnostic efficiency (AUCC: 0.628 vs. 0.458, absolute gain +0.170). These results demonstrate that proactive questioning strategy selection substantially improves the efficiency of automated SLD trait assessment, with direct implications for scalable AI-assisted clinical screening.
2605.22993.paper_summary_zh: 特徵性語言行為與自閉症譜系障礙中的社交語言障礙（SLD）相關，包括回聲重複、代名詞置換和刻板的媒體引用，通常在自發性對話中缺失，僅在特定的對話條件下出現。 在結構化的臨床評估中，這種延遲意味著提問策略的選擇是一個關鍵但未被充分重視的因素，影響對話所產生的診斷信息量。 大型語言模型（LLMs）是否能夠被指導主動選擇系統性地揭示這些潛在特徵的提問策略仍然大多未被探索。 在此，我們提出TPA（思考、計劃、詢問），這是一個主動的多代理對話框架，應用於自閉症診斷觀察時間表模塊4（ADOS-2）的語言評估部分，其中醫生代理明確推理哪些特徵尚未被觀察，然後選擇臨床基礎的策略並生成針對性的問題。 一個基於真實ADOS-2臨床數據的患者代理使得在沒有真實患者參與的情況下進行可重複的評估成為可能，並在三個獨立實驗中驗證了對真實患者語言的充分忠實度。 在對35名患者的484個案例進行評估時，TPA在所有主要指標上超越了六個競爭對話規劃基準，實現了82.1%的SLD特徵覆蓋率，比由受過訓練的臨床醫生進行的真實臨床對話的自動重播高出16.6%（65.5%），每輪診斷效率顯著提高（AUCC: 0.628 vs. 0.458，絕對增益 +0.170）。 這些結果表明，主動的提問策略選擇顯著提高了自動SLD特徵評估的效率，對可擴展的AI輔助臨床篩查具有直接的影響。
2605.22993.author: Chuanbo Hu et.al.
2605.22993.authors: Chuanbo Hu, Minglei Yin, Bin Liu, Wenqi Li, Lynn K. Paul, Shuo Wang, Xin Li
